what_was_built: reports/arbitrage_data_contract_v1/data_contract.md (human-readable contract; 5 categories + timestamp + cost + liquidity + data-quality + MVT + future-allowed + forbidden + future-artifacts + PASS/WATCH/FAIL + safety boundaries + no-profit-claim policy)
tests/test_arbitrage_data_contract.py (16 tests covering schema + safety + integration)
tools/strategy_candidate_registry.py: extra_files extended to include the data-contract docs (arbitrage lane stays IDEA)
what_the_data_contract_defines: Per-category data requirements for all five Arbitrage Research Protocol v1 categories
Timestamp + synchronization rules (UTC primary clock, 250ms default max skew, stale-quote rule, cross-venue/triangular/spot-perp alignment, row-invalidity criteria)
Cost model requirements (maker/taker fees, spread cost, depth-aware slippage, funding, borrow, withdrawal/deposit/network fees, transfer-delay risk, capital lockup)
Liquidity/depth requirements (min notional depth, depth-at-size, volume filter, stale order-book filter, quote-flicker filter, outage flags, suspicious-data flags)
Order-book / trade-print / quote requirements (L1 minimum, L2 preferred, invalidation rules)
Data quality + normalization rules (duplicates, missing, OOO, outliers, reproducibility, provenance per row, symbol canonicalization, continuous-contract stitching, split/dividend adjustment)
Minimum viable dataset (IS/OOS sealed before run, warmup buffer, fee schedule attached, outage calendar attached, provenance per row)
Future allowed data sources + forbidden data sources / methods
Required future artifacts (acquisition memo, integrity report, cost-model snapshot, stress overlays, backtest reports, closeout memo)
PASS/WATCH/FAIL rules + safety boundaries + no-profit-claim policy

tests_run.command: python -m pytest tests/test_arbitrage_data_contract.py tests/test_arbitrage_research_protocol.py tests/test_strategy_candidate_registry.py tests/test_strategy_next_bundle.py --rootdir=tests -q

guardrails_held: lane is not promoted to ACTIVE just because additional docs exist
lane never reaches STRONG evidence in the auto-build path

next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.title: Arbitrage research protocol
next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.note: Deterministic logic was NOT artificially modified. The queue at HEAD does not yet include a 'Dataset Manifest' or 'QA Harness Spec' item, so the existing protocol/data-first scoring axes still rank arbitrage at the top. Operator should consult report.md `recommended_next_bundle` for the actual Bundle 6 choice.

how_it_fits_after_arbitrage_research_pro: Arbitrage Research Protocol v1 (30dabc4, phase P0_protocol) -> Arbitrage Data Contract v1 (this bundle, phase P1_data_contract).

Subsequent phases: Dataset Manifest v1 / QA Harness Spec v1 / P2 data acquisition / P3 offline replay / P4 OOS / P5 decision gate. EACH is a separately-authorized bundle. None of them run live or paper-order execution.
recommended_next_bundle: Arbitrage Dataset Manifest v1 (one category at a time; names venue(s), pairs, fee tier, withdrawal-latency, IS/OOS windows, storage path; spec only, no data pull)
OR Arbitrage QA Harness Spec v1 (defines the harness that will verify the future acquired dataset against this contract)
OR Crypto-D1 Protocol Memo (alternative lane, currently PARKED; would require a new hypothesis, not a re-tune)
